summary: No-ship. The RED record matches its locked tree and exercises the real `run_once` seam, but two preservation premises fail. Simpler alternative: validate a single-fd directory-guard design directly on the deployed PVC before adding the broader witness machinery. Open question: does the live virtiofs mount guarantee that an open directory fd prevents inode reuse?

findings.title: B-7 is a current deployment risk, not a hypothetical migration residual
findings.body: The plan assumes the homelab PVC is ext4 while admitting that failure to pin server-side identity silently restores P-12. However, `docs/plans/2026-06-30-files-design.md:139` documents `/data` as backed by the `bulk-ssd` virtiofs bind. Virtiofs is absent from the ext4/tmpfs/overlayfs/APFS measurements. Therefore the load-bearing identity proof is unvalidated on the documented production filesystem; if its open handle does not prevent inode reuse, a replacement `.objects` can forge `(dev, ino)`, mint `Absent`, and permit `Ok` plus pending-ledger publication.
findings.recommendation: Repeat the pin/no-pin ABA and inode-reuse measurements on the mounted production PVC and make the result an acceptance/deployment gate. If virtiofs lacks the required guarantee, replace this proof with an identity mechanism explicitly supported and tested there.

findings.title: The guard creates a new EMFILE pass-abort path
findings.body: `capture_dir` retains one directory fd and then `tokio::fs::read_dir` opens another. The RED implementation needs only the latter. With exactly one descriptor available below `RLIMIT_NOFILE`, the old pass can snapshot successfully, while the proposed implementation consumes that descriptor for `DirGuard` and aborts at `read_dir` with `EMFILE`. This contradicts the plan's “new failure class 0” claim and introduces a second observable failure that can again stall GC under descriptor pressure.
findings.recommendation: Use one opened directory handle for both enumeration and identity pinning, or specify a conservative EMFILE fallback that drops the guard and retries using legacy fail-closed behavior. Add an isolated low-`RLIMIT_NOFILE` subprocess witness.

next_steps: Validate directory-handle pinning on the documented virtiofs-backed production volume.
Revise the snapshot design so it does not require an additional simultaneous descriptor.
Update the plan and rerun the adversarial plan gate.
